description:
Got a weird one. /home/user/project has a Makefile that's supposed to build three targets — `lib`, `app`, and `docs` — and there's a `make all` that should build them in dependency order. Thing is, `make -j4 all` deadlocks maybe 60% of the time, but `make all` (sequential) always works. And `make -j4 lib app docs` typed out explicitly also works fine, every time.

So it's not the individual recipes, it's something about how `all` interacts with parallel make. I've stared at the dependency declarations for an hour and they look correct to me. There's a .PHONY line, deps look right, no cycles that I can see.

Need `make -j4 all` to reliably complete. The build artifacts should end up the same as the sequential build — lib/libfoo.a, bin/app, docs/manual.html.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/project contains:
  - Makefile (writable)
  - src/foo.c, src/bar.c, src/main.c (simple C files)
  - include/foo.h (header)
  - docs/manual.md (markdown source)
- gcc, ar, and pandoc are installed.
- The Makefile contains:

```makefile
.PHONY: all lib app docs clean

all: lib app docs

lib: lib/libfoo.a

lib/libfoo.a: src/foo.o src/bar.o
	@mkdir -p lib
	ar rcs $@ $^

app: bin/app

bin/app: src/main.o lib/libfoo.a
	@mkdir -p bin
	gcc -o $@ src/main.o -Llib -lfoo

docs: docs/manual.html

docs/manual.html: docs/manual.md
	pandoc -o $@ $<

src/%.o: src/%.c include/foo.h
	gcc -c -Iinclude -o $@ $<

clean:
	rm -rf lib bin src/*.o docs/manual.html
```

- The bug: The phony target `app` depends on the phony target `lib` implicitly via `bin/app: ... lib/libfoo.a`, but there's a subtle issue. When make runs `all: lib app docs` in parallel:
  1. make starts the `lib` phony target
  2. make ALSO starts the `app` phony target simultaneously (since `all`'s prerequisites are independent phonies)
  3. `app` triggers `bin/app` which needs `lib/libfoo.a`
  4. Meanwhile `lib` triggers `lib/libfoo.a` 
  5. Both paths race to build `lib/libfoo.a` — the `ar` command and the `gcc` link step can interfere, or gcc starts linking before ar finishes

- The deeper issue: `app` (phony) does NOT declare dependency on `lib` (phony). It declares dependency on `bin/app` which depends on `lib/libfoo.a`. But make sees `lib` and `app` as independent at the phony level and starts both in parallel. The file-level dependency isn't checked until AFTER both phony targets have started their dependency resolution.

- The explicit `make -j4 lib app docs` works because make processes command-line targets left-to-right with implicit ordering, but `all`'s prerequisites have no such ordering.

- The fix: Either (a) make `app` depend on `lib` at the phony level: `app: lib bin/app` and change `app: bin/app` to `app: lib`, then add the actual rule, OR (b) use order-only prerequisites on the phony targets, OR (c) restructure so `all` depends on the real files not the phonies. The cleanest fix is `app: lib` added as a phony-level dependency.

Expected final state:
- `cd /home/user/project && make clean && make -j4 all` exits 0 (run 10 times, all must succeed).
- After successful build:
  - lib/libfoo.a exists and is a valid ar archive containing foo.o and bar.o
  - bin/app exists, is executable, and runs without "library not found" errors
  - docs/manual.html exists and is valid HTML
- `make clean && make all` (sequential) still works.

Invariants:
- src/*.c, include/foo.h, docs/manual.md unchanged.
- The Makefile still uses the same basic structure (phony targets lib/app/docs, real targets lib/libfoo.a, bin/app, docs/manual.html).
- The build outputs are identical between parallel and sequential builds.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The Makefile must still support `make -j4` (cannot add .NOTPARALLEL).
- Cannot remove the phony targets and restructure to only use file targets (the lib/app/docs phony names must remain as usable targets).
- `make -j4 all` must be run in a loop 10 times by the grader; all 10 must succeed (rules out "it works sometimes" non-fixes).
name: task_000000_30a3bd88
